---
title: "Parking the Vélib is the hard part"
subtitle: "A phone compass that points to the nearest dock with a free spot — Paris"
author: "Jonathan Jayes"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, theme.scss]
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    transition: fade
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    hash: true
    header-includes: |
      <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://unpkg.com/leaflet@1.9.4/dist/leaflet.css"/>
      <script src="https://unpkg.com/leaflet@1.9.4/dist/leaflet.js"></script>
---

## Where this started {background-color="#fcfcfc"}

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="58%"}
I cross Paris by Vélib almost daily, and the riding is the easy part. The gamble is parking. The station nearest to wherever I'm going is so often full that I end up circling the block, hunting for an open dock.

What I really wanted was one answer on my phone: which station near me has a free dock right now, and which way do I walk to reach it?
:::

::: {.column width="42%"}
![](assets/cat.gif){width="90%" style="border-radius:12px;"}

<span class="muted" style="font-size:.6em">…me, after circling the block three times.</span>
:::

:::

## It turns out I only need two feeds

Once I framed it as a question about coordinates, the whole thing got small. I need to know where the open docks are, and I need to know where the person is standing.

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
The first feed is Vélib Métropole's public GBFS status — every station's position, capacity, and how many docks are open at this moment. For the demo I ship twenty stations offline so nothing breaks on stage.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
The second is Google's Geocoding API, which turns a typed or spoken address into a `lat, lng` pair. That is the only call that leaves the phone, and the key is restricted to geocoding alone.
:::

:::

Everything else — distance, bearing, which station is nearest — is just arithmetic on two lists of coordinates.

## The phone does the arithmetic; the server only geocodes

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["📱 iPhone (React SPA)"] -->|"address string"| B["FastAPI /api/location/geocode"]
  B -->|"lat, lng"| A
  A -->|"haversine + bearing<br/>vs. 20 stations"| A
  A -->|"webkitCompassHeading"| N["🧭 needle = bearing − heading"]
  subgraph Client [runs entirely on device]
    A
    N
  end
  style Client fill:#DEEBF7,stroke:#2b8cbe
```

::: {style="font-size:.7em"}
The only thing that ever leaves the phone is the address I type. Choosing the nearest dock and turning the compass both happen on the device, which keeps it fast and, almost by accident, private.
:::

## Finding the nearest free dock, computed live

<div id="pmap" style="height:640px; border-radius:10px; border:1px solid #e6e6e6;"></div>

```{=html}
<script>
(function () {
  var stations = [{"name":"Gare de Lyon","lat":48.8442,"lng":2.3732},{"name":"Châtelet - Les Halles","lat":48.8586,"lng":2.3463},{"name":"Notre-Dame","lat":48.8534,"lng":2.3488},{"name":"Saint-Michel","lat":48.852,"lng":2.3455},{"name":"Louvre - Rivoli","lat":48.8606,"lng":2.3376},{"name":"Pigalle","lat":48.8841,"lng":2.3417},{"name":"Montparnasse","lat":48.8423,"lng":2.3231},{"name":"Opéra Garnier","lat":48.8719,"lng":2.3515},{"name":"Gare du Nord","lat":48.8808,"lng":2.355},{"name":"Eiffel Tower","lat":48.8584,"lng":2.2945},{"name":"Luxembourg","lat":48.8467,"lng":2.3374},{"name":"République","lat":48.8692,"lng":2.363},{"name":"Bastille","lat":48.8534,"lng":2.369},{"name":"Marais - Saint-Paul","lat":48.8545,"lng":2.3636},{"name":"Le Marais - Rambuteau","lat":48.862,"lng":2.358},{"name":"Les Invalides","lat":48.8611,"lng":2.3125},{"name":"Arc de Triomphe","lat":48.8738,"lng":2.295},{"name":"Père Lachaise","lat":48.8686,"lng":2.3934},{"name":"Buttes-Chaumont","lat":48.8858,"lng":2.3886},{"name":"Bercy","lat":48.8358,"lng":2.3789}];
  var dest = {name:"21 rue des Gravilliers", lat:48.8615, lng:2.3636};

  function haversine(a, b) {
    var R=6371000, toRad=Math.PI/180;
    var dLat=(b.lat-a.lat)*toRad, dLng=(b.lng-a.lng)*toRad;
    var s=Math.sin(dLat/2)*Math.sin(dLat/2) + Math.cos(a.lat*toRad)*Math.cos(b.lat*toRad)*Math.sin(dLng/2)*Math.sin(dLng/2);
    return 2*R*Math.asin(Math.sqrt(s));
  }

  var map, built=false;
  function build() {
    if (built || typeof L === "undefined" || !document.getElementById("pmap")) return;
    built = true;
    map = L.map("pmap", {scrollWheelZoom:false}).setView([dest.lat, dest.lng], 14);
    L.tileLayer("https://{s}.tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png",
      {attribution:"© OpenStreetMap", maxZoom:19}).addTo(map);

    var nearest=null, best=Infinity;
    stations.forEach(function(s){ var d=haversine(dest,s); if(d<best){best=d; nearest=s;} });

    stations.forEach(function(s){
      var isNear = s===nearest;
      L.circleMarker([s.lat,s.lng], {
        radius:isNear?11:6,
        color:isNear?"#469408":"#2b8cbe",
        weight:isNear?3:1, fillColor:isNear?"#469408":"#2b8cbe",
        fillOpacity:isNear?0.9:0.55
      }).addTo(map).bindTooltip(s.name + (isNear?" — nearest ("+Math.round(best)+" m)":""));
    });

    L.marker([dest.lat,dest.lng]).addTo(map)
      .bindTooltip("🎯 " + dest.name, {permanent:true, direction:"top"});
    L.polyline([[dest.lat,dest.lng],[nearest.lat,nearest.lng]],
      {color:"#469408", weight:3, dashArray:"6 6"}).addTo(map);

    setTimeout(function(){ map.invalidateSize(); }, 200);
  }

  if (document.readyState !== "loading") build();
  else document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", build);
  if (window.Reveal) {
    Reveal.on("ready", function(){ build(); if(map) map.invalidateSize(); });
    Reveal.on("slidechanged", function(){ build(); if(map) setTimeout(function(){map.invalidateSize();},150); });
  }
})();
</script>
```

::: {style="font-size:.6em"}
Destination 21 rue des Gravilliers, green is the nearest open dock, blue the rest. This is the same haversine that runs on the phone.
:::

## Getting a true-north compass out of an iPhone

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="52%"}
```js
// Must be called *inside* a user gesture (a button tap)
await DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission();

function onOrient(e) {
  // iOS: this is TRUE north, clockwise.
  // NOT e.alpha (relative + inverted).
  const heading = e.webkitCompassHeading
                ?? (360 - e.alpha);   // Android
}
```
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}
This was the part that quietly cost me an afternoon. There are three ways to get nothing at all: without HTTPS the sensors and geolocation are blocked outright; ask for permission on page load and the prompt is simply ignored, because iOS needs a real tap; and if you read `e.alpha` you get an arbitrary origin instead of north.

The fix that finally worked was an "Enable Compass" button that calls `requestPermission()` on the tap, and then reads `webkitCompassHeading`.
:::

:::

## One container on Cloud Run, and HTTPS comes free

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  S["git push"] --> CB["Cloud Build"]
  CB --> IMG["Artifact Registry<br/>single image"]
  IMG --> CR["Cloud Run<br/>europe-west9 · HTTPS"]
  CR --> P["📱 *.run.app"]
  SM["Secret Manager<br/>Mistral · Geocoding"] -.-> CR
  style CR fill:#DEEBF7,stroke:#2b8cbe
```

::: {style="font-size:.68em"}
I package everything as one image: FastAPI serves both the built React app and the API from a single origin, so there is no CORS to fight, and every `*.run.app` URL is HTTPS by default — which is exactly what the iPhone compass was insisting on. The keys stay in Secret Manager.
:::

## Three ways in, then you just follow the needle

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}
You can reach a destination three ways. Speak it, and Mistral Vox transcribes while a Mistral model pulls the address out of what you said. Type it into the collapsible sidebar. Or hit demo, which drops you at 21 rue des Gravilliers in a single tap.

From there the compass points at the nearest open dock, and once you've parked, a tap on "Parked" flips the needle toward your actual destination. Your position updates live through `watchPosition`.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
<iframe src="https://velib-app-c3eayx2yta-od.a.run.app"
        style="width:360px; height:640px; border:10px solid #222; border-radius:34px; background:#fff;"
        loading="lazy"></iframe>
:::

:::

## Where it stands, and what's next

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
It's live today: a single container on Cloud Run, served over HTTPS at [velib-app…run.app](https://velib-app-c3eayx2yta-od.a.run.app), with real geocoding, a working iOS compass, and voice, typed, and demo input all in place.

What I'd reach for next is swapping the twenty-station demo set for the live GBFS feed, ranking docks by walking time rather than straight-line distance, and turning it into an installable app with offline map tiles.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](assets/cat-2.gif){width="95%" style="border-radius:12px;"}

<span class="muted" style="font-size:.6em">Merci — questions?</span>
:::

:::
